title: The Sea Is Full Of Wandering Foam
content:
The sea is full of wandering foam,
    The sky of driving cloud;
    My restless thoughts among them roam . . .
    The night is dark and loud.

    Where are the hours that came to me
    So beautiful and bright?
    A wild wind shakes the wilder sea . . .
    O, dark and loud's the night!

    1876
meaning:
This evocative piece by William Ernest Henley, titled "The Sea Is Full Of Wandering Foam", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
